page: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical Intel Xeon cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz, the x86_64 architecture processor includes a 54 MB L3 cache. The instance is configured with 976 GB of dedicated RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 15.25 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory-intensive capabilities. The server achieved a Passmark CPU score of 55,058.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 16,654.0, both placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 3,290.07 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 151.0 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Database operations performed strongly at 26,171.33 on Passmark, while static web serving throughput reached 43.05 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m3-megamem-64 offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for workloads requiring massive RAM footprints without the overhead of additional CPU cores. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy operations.

This instance is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, heavy caching layers, and memory-bound data processing pipelines. It is also capable of handling software compilation and moderate LLM inference tasks, as demonstrated by its strong performance in Clang compilation and 7B model text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server configured with 64 vCPUs and 976 GB of RAM, providing 15.25 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz with a 54 MB L3 cache, this instance features dedicated CPU allocation but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 55,058.84 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 16,654.0. It excels in memory-intensive tasks, demonstrating a cached read bandwidth of 3,290.07 GB/sec and strong database operations. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads, though the absence of local storage requires network-attached alternatives. This server is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-intensive data processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 976 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads and large-scale database operations.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform m3-megamem-64 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 976 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 976 GB of RAM and 64 vCPUs for demanding in-memory databases and data processing.

976 GB of dedicated RAM providing 15.25 GB per core
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
No local storage or bundled GPU accelerators
Optimized for in-memory databases and large-scale caching
